name: Domates Dolmast
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut thin slices from the stem end of each tomato; reserve slices.
2. Set a sieve over a bowl. Scoop out pulp from tomatoes and place pulp in sieve to drain; set shells upside down on paper towels and let drain. Discard seeds from tomato pulp; reserve liquid.
3. Chop the pulp and set aside.
4. In a small nonstick skillet, heat 1 teaspoon olive oil. Add onion and garlic and sauté until onion is golden.
5. Stir in currants, salt, pepper, and 2 tablespoons of reserved tomato liquid. Remove from heat and stir in rice, nuts, parsley, mint, and sunflower seeds.
6. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
7. In an 8 x 8 x 2-inch baking dish, set tomato shells upright; spoon 1/4 of the rice mixture into each tomato and top each with a reserved tomato slice.
8. Brush 1/2 teaspoon oil over each tomato and pour remaining tomato liquid into the baking dish (to prevent sticking). Sprinkle each tomato with 3/4 teaspoon bread crumbs.
9. Bake until filling is hot and skin begins to split, about 30 minutes. Makes 4 servings.
